# introgression.ReferenceQC { #tspaint.introgression.ReferenceQC }

```python
tspaint.introgression.ReferenceQC(
    labels,
    credibility,
    loo_agreement,
    learned_w,
    anchors,
    maps,
    Q,
    pi,
    _length=0.0,
    sample_ids=None,
    individual_ids=None,
)
```

Result of :func:`reference_qc` — a per-reference admixture/mislabel audit.

## Attributes {.doc-section .doc-section-attributes}

<code>[**labels**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[dict](`dict`)\[[int](`int`), [int](`int`)\]]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Reference **sample-node (haplotype) index** -> its nominal ancestry-state label. Keys are haplotype nodes even when :func:`reference_qc` was called with base-id / individual labels, because credibility is learned per tip (per haplotype). Use :attr:`individual_ids` / :attr:`sample_ids` (or :meth:`summary`) to read them back as the ids you passed in.

<code>[**credibility**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[dict](`dict`)\[[int](`int`), [float](`float`)\]]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Per-reference credibility in ``[0, 1]`` — the learned ``w_i`` for softened (suspect) references, the leave-one-out self-agreement for the hard-clamped anchor core. Low = the genealogy disagrees with the label (admixed / mislabelled).

<code>[**loo_agreement**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[dict](`dict`)\[[int](`int`), [float](`float`)\]]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Pass-1 leave-one-out self-agreement for every reference (the hard-clamp baseline).

<code>[**learned_w**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[dict](`dict`)\[[int](`int`), [float](`float`)\]]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Learned credibility ``w_i`` for the softened references only.

<code>[**anchors**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[set](`set`)\[[int](`int`)\]]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   References kept hard-clamped as the trusted core (CLAUDE.md §6).

<code>[**maps**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[dict](`dict`)\[[int](`int`), [list](`list`)\]]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Per reference, its leave-one-out introgression map (:func:`tspaint.output.loo_posterior_table` segments) — where its own genealogy dissents from its label.

<code>[**Q, pi**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[numpy](`numpy`).[ndarray](`numpy.ndarray`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   The fitted generator and root frequencies (from the refined pass when refining).

<code>[**sample_ids**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[dict](`dict`)\[[int](`int`), [str](`str`)\] or None]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Per-reference haplotype-node -> its stamped **haplotype** id (e.g. ``"PD_0213_1"``), when the tree sequence carried sample names (from :func:`tspaint.io.tsinfer` / ``singer`` / ``relate``); ``None`` for an unstamped (bare-sim) tree sequence.

<code>[**individual_ids**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[dict](`dict`)\[[int](`int`), [str](`str`)\] or None]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Per-reference haplotype-node -> its **individual** id (e.g. ``"PD_0213"``) — a diploid reference's two haplotype nodes share one individual id. Lets :meth:`summary` / :meth:`soft_refs` speak in the ids you passed in rather than raw node indices.

## Methods

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| [flagged_tracts](#tspaint.introgression.ReferenceQC.flagged_tracts) | Foreign tracts of ``ref``: where the genealogy confidently prefers a non-label state. |
| [foreign_fraction](#tspaint.introgression.ReferenceQC.foreign_fraction) | Fraction of ``ref``'s genome flagged foreign at the given ``deadband``. |
| [introgression_map](#tspaint.introgression.ReferenceQC.introgression_map) | The leave-one-out introgression map (list of segments) for one reference. |
| [mask](#tspaint.introgression.ReferenceQC.mask) | Per-reference foreign spans to **mask** out before re-painting. |
| [soft_refs](#tspaint.introgression.ReferenceQC.soft_refs) | References to soften when re-painting — feed straight to ``paint(..., soft_refs=...)``. |
| [summary](#tspaint.introgression.ReferenceQC.summary) | Per-reference-**haplotype** QC rows, least-credible first (the audit table). |

### flagged_tracts { #tspaint.introgression.ReferenceQC.flagged_tracts }

```python
tspaint.introgression.ReferenceQC.flagged_tracts(
    ref,
    deadband=DEFAULT_QC_DEADBAND,
)
```

Foreign tracts of ``ref``: where the genealogy confidently prefers a non-label state.

A segment is flagged where ``loo[foreign] - loo[label] >= deadband`` (``foreign`` =
the best non-label state). Returns merged ``[(left, right, foreign_state)]``.

### foreign_fraction { #tspaint.introgression.ReferenceQC.foreign_fraction }

```python
tspaint.introgression.ReferenceQC.foreign_fraction(
    ref,
    deadband=DEFAULT_QC_DEADBAND,
)
```

Fraction of ``ref``'s genome flagged foreign at the given ``deadband``.

### introgression_map { #tspaint.introgression.ReferenceQC.introgression_map }

```python
tspaint.introgression.ReferenceQC.introgression_map(ref)
```

The leave-one-out introgression map (list of segments) for one reference.

### mask { #tspaint.introgression.ReferenceQC.mask }

```python
tspaint.introgression.ReferenceQC.mask(deadband=DEFAULT_QC_DEADBAND)
```

Per-reference foreign spans to **mask** out before re-painting.

The alternative Task-1 action to :meth:`soft_refs`: rather than down-weight a whole
reference, drop only its contaminated spans. Returns ``{ref: [(left, right), ...]}`` from
:meth:`flagged_tracts` (foreign state dropped) for the references that have at least one
flagged tract.

### soft_refs { #tspaint.introgression.ReferenceQC.soft_refs }

```python
tspaint.introgression.ReferenceQC.soft_refs(max_credibility=None, by='node')
```

References to soften when re-painting — feed straight to ``paint(..., soft_refs=...)``.

The Task-1 action: down-weight the contaminated references so they stop anchoring the
painting where they carry foreign ancestry. With ``max_credibility=None`` (default) returns
the suspect set the QC already softened (the non-anchor references); pass a cutoff to take
every reference with ``credibility < max_credibility`` instead. Keep the trusted anchors
hard-clamped — never let the whole panel float (CLAUDE.md §6).

#### Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

<code>[**max_credibility**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[float](`float`)]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [None]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Cutoff (see above); default returns the QC's softened (non-anchor) set.

<code>[**by**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [(\'node\', \'individual\')]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [\"node\"]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   ``"node"`` (default) returns sample-**node** indices; ``"individual"`` returns the distinct **individual** ids (e.g. ``"PD_0213"``) — more legible, and still accepted by ``paint(..., soft_refs=...)`` (which resolves ids). ``"individual"`` requires a stamped tree sequence; a diploid individual is included if *either* of its haplotypes is suspect.

#### Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

<code>[]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[set](`set`)\[[int](`int`)\] or [set](`set`)\[[str](`str`)\]]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Node indices (``by="node"``) or individual ids (``by="individual"``) to pass as ``soft_refs``.

### summary { #tspaint.introgression.ReferenceQC.summary }

```python
tspaint.introgression.ReferenceQC.summary(deadband=DEFAULT_QC_DEADBAND)
```

Per-reference-**haplotype** QC rows, least-credible first (the audit table).

One row per reference haplotype (two per diploid individual — credibility is learned per
tip). ``ref`` is the sample-node index; when the tree sequence carried sample names the row
also names the ``individual`` (base id) and ``haplotype`` (per-haplotype id) so the table is
legible in your own ids, not just node integers.
